title: Allowing browsers to perform DNS prefetching is security-sensitive
description: By default, web browsers perform DNS prefetching to reduce latency due to DNS resolutions required when an user clicks links from a website page. For instance on example.com the hyperlink below contains a cross-origin domain name that must be resolved to an IP address by the web browser: go on our partner website It can add significant latency during requests, especially if the page contains many links to cross-origin domains. DNS prefetch allows web browsers to perform DNS resolving in the background before the user clicks a link.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: DNS prefetching is enabled which may pose security risks. Consider disabling or reviewing the configuration.
